zoning.code: El Cerrito Municipal Code Title 19 (Zoning), §19.06.030 Development Standards
zoning.tallPortionSetbacks: portions of building >25 ft tall require 10 ft side setback (and 20 ft rear when abutting an RS zone)

heightMeasurement.topReference: highest point of the structure (i.e. the roof ridge / top of covering) — NOT a midpoint; no sloped-roof averaging
heightMeasurement.gradeDatum: ground level directly below, at the lower of natural or finished grade (per part B); not an averaged datum
heightMeasurement.allParts: all parts of the building except listed projections must comply with the max height

notes: RM has NO daylight-plane requirement (unlike the RS districts); height is governed by the flat 35 ft cap.

Per §19.03.050, height is measured to the HIGHEST point of the roof from the ground directly below (lower of natural/finished grade) — no midpoint averaging, and the 2.5 ft crawlspace counts (it sits above grade=z=0).

DESIGN: raised plate (story-and-a-half), 2.5 ft attic perimeter wall -> eave at 25.0 ft AG (overhang fascia ~24.2 ft). Roof 8:12; structural ridge ~34.33 ft, top of covering ~34.96 ft — under the 35 ft cap but by only ~0.5 in. CAUTION: at 8:12 there is essentially no margin; confirm actual roof-assembly thickness or back off to ~7.75:12 for a safe ~3 in. The south shed dormer is a FLAT roof behind a 2 ft decorated parapet (coping + cornice + dentils), which stays well below the ridge.

Eave wall held AT 25 ft (not over), so the building walls do not trigger RM's >25 ft side/rear setback step; the roof + dormers rise above 25 ft, so confirm how the city applies the >25 ft rule to roof vs wall portions (ECMC §19.06.030 table).
8:12 yields ~820 sf >=5 ft and ~564 sf >=7 ft of attic floor (vs 643/439 for the eave-at-floor 10:12 scheme); the flat shed dormer gives a 7.5 ft clear ceiling over its bay.
Remaining to confirm: roof-assembly thickness keeps the ridge <35 ft; any RM story/floor-area cap; parcel designation 'RM5' shares the base RM standards in the §19.06.030 table.
